title.en: Support to Training and Employment Programme for Women (STEP)
title.hi: महिलाओं के लिए प्रशिक्षण और रोजगार सहायता कार्यक्रम (STEP)

description.en: Under the Ministry of Women and Child Development, it aims to provide skill training and employment linkages to poor and asset-less women in traditional sectors like agriculture, dairying, and handlooms.

benefits.en: Free vocational skill training, access to credit, support services (child care and healthcare), and forward/backward integration.

en: Step 1: Locate an NGO or cooperative implementing STEP projects in your area.
Step 2: Submit admission form at the training agency.
Step 3: Complete the training and receive certificate/employment assistance.
Step 1: Locate an NGO or cooperative implementing STEP projects in your area.
Step 2: Submit admission form at the training agency.
Step 3: Complete the training and receive certificate/employment assistance.

description.en: Managed by the Centre for Cultural Resources and Training (CCRT) to identify and support young talented children in artistic fields (music, dance, drama, etc.) by providing specialized training and funding.

benefits.en: Scholarship amount of ₹3,600 per year, and reimbursement of tuition fee up to ₹9,000 per year for specialized training.

en: Step 1: Register on the National Scholarships Portal (NSP) or CCRT portal.
Step 2: Fill out the application and submit portfolio/receipt of training.
Step 3: Selection is done via screening and audition/interview.
Step 1: Register on the National Scholarships Portal (NSP) or CCRT portal.
Step 2: Fill out the application and submit portfolio/receipt of training.
Step 3: Selection is done via screening and audition/interview.

description.en: A credit-linked subsidy programme by Ministry of MSME to generate self-employment opportunities through setting up new micro-enterprises in non-farm sectors.

benefits.en: Bank loan with government margin money subsidy ranging from 15% to 35% (up to 35% margin money subsidy for special categories like women, SC/ST, rural areas). Project cost up to ₹50 Lakhs for manufacturing.

documentsRequired: Educational Certificate (VIII Pass minimum for >10 Lakh project)

en: Step 1: Visit the PMEGP e-Portal run by KVIC.
Step 2: Fill online application and upload project report.
Step 3: Application is forwarded to bank for loan sanctioning and KVIC for subsidy.

amount: Up to 35% subsidy on ₹50 Lakh project

en: Step 1: Visit the PMEGP e-Portal run by KVIC.
Step 2: Fill online application and upload project report.
Step 3: Application is forwarded to bank for loan sanctioning and KVIC for subsidy.

amountLocale.en: Up to 35% subsidy on ₹50 Lakh project

description.en: Provides financial assistance to startups for Proof of Concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization.

benefits.en: Grants up to ₹20 Lakhs for proof of concept, and up to ₹50 Lakhs seed funding through debt or convertible debentures.

en: Step 1: Go to seedfund.startupindia.gov.in.
Step 2: Submit details of your recognized startup and upload pitch deck.
Step 3: Select preferred incubators for evaluation and funding approval.
Step 1: Go to seedfund.startupindia.gov.in.
Step 2: Submit details of your recognized startup and upload pitch deck.
Step 3: Select preferred incubators for evaluation and funding approval.

title.en: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)
title.hi: इंदिरा गांधी राष्ट्रीय विधवा पेंशन योजना

description.en: Centrally sponsored scheme by Ministry of Rural Development to provide pension security to widows from BPL families.

benefits.en: Monthly pension of ₹300/- per month for widows aged 40 to 79. The amount increases to ₹500/- per month from age 80 onwards.

en: Step 1: Visit web.umang.gov.in/web_new/home or open UMANG App.
Step 2: Search for NSAP and click 'Apply Online'.
Step 3: Enter details, upload photo and submit.
Step 1: Visit web.umang.gov.in/web_new/home or open UMANG App.
Step 2: Search for NSAP and click 'Apply Online'.
Step 3: Enter details, upload photo and submit.

description.en: Implemented by Government of Himachal Pradesh to provide livelihood pension and economic support to poor widows, abandoned women, and unmarried women.

benefits.en: Monthly pension of ₹1,150/- disbursed directly to the bank account.

en: Step 1: Obtain application form from Gram Panchayat or block development office.
Step 2: Fill out form and attach required certificates.
Step 3: Submit completed form to Block Development Office.
Step 1: Obtain application form from Gram Panchayat or block development office.
Step 2: Fill out form and attach required certificates.
Step 3: Submit completed form to Block Development Office.

title.hi: मणिपुर गरीब विधवा वित्तीय सहायता योजना

description.en: A scheme by Department of Social Welfare, Manipur, providing social security to widows in the age group of 18-40 years who are not covered under IGNWPS.

benefits.en: Financial assistance at the rate of ₹2,000/- per annum per widow, subject to fund availability.

en: Step 1: Download application form from socialwelfare.nic.in or collect from CDPO/DSWO.
Step 2: Fill details and attach self-attested documents.
Step 3: Submit the form to the District Social Welfare Officer concerned.
Step 1: Download application form from socialwelfare.nic.in or collect from CDPO/DSWO.
Step 2: Fill details and attach self-attested documents.
Step 3: Submit the form to the District Social Welfare Officer concerned.

description.en: Digital Complaint Registration and referral system for women affected by violence or in distress across India, run from the premises of the National Commission for Women, New Delhi.

benefits.en: 24/7 telephonic/digital counselling, psychological support, and referrals to police, hospitals, and crisis centers.

en: Step 1: Go to ncwwomenhelpline.ncw.gov.in or dial the short-code 181.
Step 2: Describe the distress issue and provide contact/location details.
Step 3: Counsellors arrange police or medical assistance instantly.
Step 1: Go to ncwwomenhelpline.ncw.gov.in or dial the short-code 181.
Step 2: Describe the distress issue and provide contact/location details.
Step 3: Counsellors arrange police or medical assistance instantly.

title.en: Relief and Rehabilitation of Acid Victims (Haryana)
title.hi: हरियाणा एसिड हमला पीड़िता राहत योजना

description.en: Launched by WCD Department, Government of Haryana, to provide financial and medical assistance to girl/women acid attack victims.

benefits.en: 100% free medical treatment at approved hospitals. Ad-hoc relief of ₹1,00,000/- (₹25,000 immediately, ₹75,000 within 15 days).

en: Step 1: Visit official Antyodaya-SARAL Portal.
Step 2: Log in using your registered credentials.
Step 3: Search for 'Relief and Rehabilitation of Women Acid Victims' in services list.
Step 4: Fill form details, upload documents, and submit.
Step 1: Visit official Antyodaya-SARAL Portal.
Step 2: Log in using your registered credentials.
Step 3: Search for 'Relief and Rehabilitation of Women Acid Victims' in services list.
Step 4: Fill form details, upload documents, and submit.

title.en: Financial Assistance to Acid Victims (Pension - Haryana)
title.hi: हरियाणा एसिड हमला पीड़िता पेंशन योजना

description.en: Social security pension scheme by Social Justice & Empowerment Department, Govt. of Haryana, providing a monthly pension based on disability percentage.

benefits.en: Monthly pension based on disability percentage: 2.5 times Divyang Pension (40-50% disability); 3.5 times (51-60% disability); 4.5 times (>61% disability).

documentsRequired: Copy of application made to WCD

en: Step 1: Visit Office of the District Social Welfare Officer (DSWO).
Step 2: Fill out application form and attach self-attested documents.
Step 3: Submit to DSWO and receive acknowledgment receipt.
Step 1: Visit Office of the District Social Welfare Officer (DSWO).
Step 2: Fill out application form and attach self-attested documents.
Step 3: Submit to DSWO and receive acknowledgment receipt.
